title: Tracaulon beyrichianum
taxonomy: Tracaulon beyrichianum is classified in the family Polygalaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the milkwort family. This family consists of around 85 genera and 1,200 species of plants. The genus Tracaulon is a small group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. Tracaulon beyrichianum is a species within this genus, with limited information available on its specific classification within the genus. Further research is needed to determine the full classification of this species.
uses: Tracaulon beyrichianum, a plant species with limited information available, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. properties: Tracaulon beyrichianum has been found to have several therapeutic properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
